meta.title: Your First Strapi Collection Type
meta.excerpt: Set up Strapi CMS and create your first collection type for managing content

props.text: Create a new Strapi project using the CLI installer.

props.code:
# Create a new Strapi project
npx create-strapi-app@latest my-cms

# When prompted:
# - Installation type: Quickstart (SQLite)
# - or Custom if you want PostgreSQL

# This will install and start Strapi
# Admin panel: http://localhost:1337/admin

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Quickstart uses SQLite which is perfect for development. For production, you'll want PostgreSQL or MySQL.

props.text: When Strapi starts, you'll be prompted to create your first admin user.

props.code:
# Visit http://localhost:1337/admin
# You'll see a registration form:

First name: [Your name]
Last name: [Your name]
Email: admin@example.com
Password: [Strong password]

# Click "Let's start" to enter the admin panel

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: This creates a super admin account. In production, you'd want to set up proper roles and permissions for your team.

props.text: Collection Types are for content you'll have multiple entries of, like blog posts or products.

props.code:
# In the Strapi admin:

1. Go to Content-Type Builder (left sidebar)
2. Click "Create new collection type"
3. Set the display name: "Contact Request"
4. API ID will auto-generate: "contact-request"
5. Click "Continue"

# Now add fields:

1. Click "Add another field"
2. Select "Text" and name it "firstName"
3. Go to Advanced Settings and check "Required"
4. Save

5. Add another "Text" field named "lastName" as Required
6. Add "Email" field named "email" as Required
7. Add "Text (Long text)" named "message" as Required
8. Add "Enumeration" named "status" with values: pending, reviewed, resolved

9. Click "Save" (top right)

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: Strapi will create the database tables and API endpoints automatically. You now have REST and GraphQL APIs ready to use.

props.text: Add content via the admin panel.

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Draft entries won't appear in the API until published. This is useful for review workflows.

props.code:
# In the Strapi admin:

1. Go to Content Manager (left sidebar)
2. Click "Contact Request"
3. Click "Create new entry"
4. Fill in the fields:
   - firstName: John
   - lastName: Doe
   - email: john@example.com
   - message: I'd like to learn more about your services
   - status: pending
5. Click "Save"
6. Click "Publish" to make it available via API

props.text: Strapi automatically creates REST endpoints for your collection types.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: By default, all endpoints are private. You need to explicitly enable access for public or authenticated roles.
